data.title: Southbound Funds Record Net Sell-off of 2.467 Billion Yuan in Hong Kong Stocks
data.description: Southbound funds experienced a net sell-off totaling 2.467 billion yuan today. According to Jin10, within the Hong Kong Stock Connect (Shanghai), the Tracker Fund of Hong Kong and Alibaba-W saw net sell-offs of 1.228 billion HKD and 780 million HKD, respectively. Tencent Holdings led in net buying, with an amount of 437 million HKD. In the Hong Kong Stock Connect (Shenzhen), the Tracker Fund of Hong Kong and Southern Hang Seng Technology recorded net sell-offs of 2.618 billion HKD and 1.338 billion HKD, respectively, while Tencent Holdings again topped net buying with 172 million HKD.

data.title: Portugal Anticipates Bids from Major Airlines for TAP Stake
data.description: Portugal is optimistic that Europe's three largest airline groups will submit bids for a stake in TAP, the national airline, as the deadline approaches this week. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's expectation that these major players will show interest in acquiring a portion of TAP. The move is part of Portugal's broader strategy to strengthen its aviation sector and ensure TAP's competitive position in the market. The anticipated bids are seen as a crucial step in the airline's future development and sustainability.

data.title: Binance Futures to Introduce USDⓈ-Margined BASEDUSDT Perpetual Contract
data.description: Binance Futures is set to launch a new USDⓈ-margined BASEDUSDT perpetual contract on March 30, 2026. According to BWEnews, this announcement marks a significant addition to the platform's offerings. The BASEDUSDT contract will allow traders to engage in perpetual trading with USDⓈ as the margin currency. The current market capitalization of BASED is approximately $34 million, although this figure may be subject to change.

data.title: STOCKS | S&P 500 Correction Approaches Final Stage Amid Ongoing Iran Conflict
data.description: Morgan Stanley strategists have indicated that the S&P 500 correction is nearing its final stage, despite the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes continue to pose a threat to stock markets. The strategists emphasize the potential impact of monetary policy on market stability, suggesting that investors remain cautious as geopolitical tensions persist.

data.title: Iran Strengthens Control Over Strait of Hormuz Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: After a month of fighting, Iran has reportedly achieved a significant strategic advantage by tightening its control over traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the geopolitical implications of this development. The Strait of Hormuz is a crucial maritime passage for global oil shipments, and Iran's increased influence in the region could impact international trade and energy markets. This strategic move comes amid ongoing tensions and military engagements in the area, raising concerns among global powers about the stability and security of this vital shipping lane.

data.title: Iran's First Vice President: If you want to negotiate on the Strait of Hormuz issue, you must promise not to invade Iran.
data.description: According to the Wall Street Journal, Iran's First Vice President Mohammad Reza Arif stated that if Iran's "aggressors" want to negotiate on the Strait of Hormuz issue, they must promise not to invade Iran and recognize Iran's international rights.

data.title: Jio Financial Services Shares Decline Amid Insurance Expansion Plans
data.description: Jio Financial Services shares have decreased to Rs. 227, down from Rs. 333.9 in early August 2025. According to NS3.AI, this decline comes as the company announces its intention to enter the life and general insurance sectors. CEO and MD Hitesh Sethia stated that the rollout is anticipated in 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

data.title: Tether Gold Launches on BNB Chain, Enhancing Blockchain Integration
data.description:
Tether Gold (XAUt), a tokenized gold product from Tether, was launched on BNB Chain on March 26. According to Foresight News, each XAUt token is backed 1:1 by a troy ounce of physical gold stored in Swiss vaults, with reserves comprising approximately 1,800 London Good Delivery gold bars, totaling over 22,100 kilograms. Proof of reserves has been publicly disclosed.

The integration allows users to access physical gold exposure within the BNB Chain ecosystem, facilitating its use in trading, DeFi, and other on-chain activities. For developers, XAUt offers an asset with low correlation to the crypto market, aiding in more balanced product design. Tether plans to enhance liquidity across more than 12 blockchains through the USDt0 cross-chain network, improving issuance, transfer, and settlement efficiency.

Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino stated that the initiative aims to incorporate gold into the modern digital financial system, enabling instant settlement and global circulation.

Currently, BNB Chain's real-world asset (RWA) scale is approximately $3.2 billion, ranking second among global RWA public chains, with over 41,000 holders and a net inflow of about $845 million in the past 30 days. The addition of XAUt signifies a shift from 'issue and store' to a new stage of usable, composable on-chain assets.

data.title: Veteran Trader Mac Shares Key Concept for Analyzing Market Structures
data.description: PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter) that veteran trader Mac, with a decade of experience, simplifies technical analysis by focusing on a single core concept: market structure. Mac claims that this approach allows him to assess the value of a candlestick chart within three seconds. He suggests that losses in trading may not be due to bad luck but rather a failure to properly analyze the charts.

data.title: Upbit's parent company, Dunamu, projected revenue of 1.56 trillion won in 2025, a 10.0% decrease year-over-year.
data.description: According to South Korean media outlet Decenter, Dunamu, the operator of Upbit, South Korea's largest cryptocurrency exchange, released its 2025 annual results, showing a decline in both revenue and profit. According to its consolidated financial statements, Dunamu's operating revenue in 2025 was 1.56 trillion won, a 10.0% decrease year-on-year (compared to 1.73 trillion won in the same period of the previous year); operating profit was 869.3 billion won, a 26.7% decrease year-on-year (compared to 1.18 trillion won in the same period of the previous year); and net profit was 708.9 billion won, a 27.9% decrease year-on-year (compared to 983.8 billion won in the same period of the previous year). The report states that the decline in performance was mainly attributed to the shrinking trading volume in the cryptocurrency market against the backdrop of a global economic downturn. Dunamu's revenue structure is highly concentrated in transaction fees, which accounted for 98.26% of total revenue in 2025.

data.title: Coinbase announced that it will launch BASED1 spot trading today.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Coinbase has announced that Based One (BASED1) spot trading will officially launch on March 30, 2026. Subject to liquidity conditions being met, the BASED1-USD trading pair will open later that day, exclusively for users in supported regions.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs See Positive Inflows Amid Promising Outlook
data.description: Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have experienced healthy inflows so far this month, according to CoinShares research analyst Alexandre Schmidt. After several weeks of outflows, the trend appears to be reversing. According to NS3.AI, Schmidt noted that while the outlook is promising, he does not anticipate major records in the second quarter. He is also monitoring Japan as a potential future market for Bitcoin ETFs.

data.title: XRP ETFs See Inflows Amid Broader Market Recovery
data.description: XRP exchange-traded funds (ETFs) attracted $2.66 million in net inflows last week, contrasting with significant outflows in Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs, which saw $296.18 million and $206.58 million respectively. According to NS3.AI, Solana ETFs also experienced a loss of $4.24 million. XRP's rebound appears to be part of a wider market recovery rather than a distinct ETF-driven surge.

data.title: Eurozone March Industrial Sentiment Index Improves Slightly
data.description: The Eurozone's industrial sentiment index for March registered at -7, slightly better than the anticipated -8, according to Jin10. The previous value was revised from -7.1 to -7.2, indicating a minor improvement in industrial confidence within the region. This data suggests a cautious optimism among industrial players, despite ongoing economic challenges.

data.title: Binance Alpha Lists BASED Token, Trading at $0.115
data.description: Binance Alpha has listed the BASED token, with trading currently priced at approximately $0.115 USDT, according to the platform’s official interface.

data.title: Eurozone Consumer Confidence Index Remains Unchanged in March
data.description: The Eurozone's consumer confidence index for March has been finalized at -16.3, matching both the forecast and the previous month's figure. According to Jin10, this stability in consumer sentiment reflects ongoing concerns about economic conditions in the region. The unchanged index suggests that consumers remain cautious amid uncertainties in the Eurozone's economic outlook.

data.title: BitMart will launch R2 Protocol (R2).
data.description: According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch R2 Protocol (R2) on March 30th at 18:30 (UTC+8), with the R2/USDT trading pair available. R2 Protocol is an on-chain asset management platform dedicated to providing institutional-grade real yields for both crypto-native and traditional users.

data.title: Digital Asset Investment Products See Significant Outflows Amid Global Tensions
data.description:
CoinShares has reported a net outflow of $414 million from digital asset investment products last week, marking the first outflow in five weeks. According to Foresight News, this decline is attributed to the ongoing conflict in Iran, inflation concerns, and expectations of interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve. The total assets under management (AuM) have decreased to $129 billion.

The negative sentiment was predominantly observed in the United States, which saw outflows of $445 million. In contrast, Germany and Canada experienced inflows of $21.2 million and $15.9 million, respectively. Ethereum was notably affected by news related to the Clarity Act, resulting in outflows of $222 million, bringing its total net outflow for the year to $273 million.

Bitcoin experienced outflows of $194 million last week, although it maintains a net inflow of $964 million for the year. Short Bitcoin investment products saw an inflow of $4 million. Solana recorded outflows of $12.3 million, while XRP had inflows of $15.8 million.

data.title: Crypto Market Faces Potential Volatility Amid Key Economic Events
data.description:
The final week of March may bring significant volatility to the cryptocurrency market. According to BlockBeats, the FTX Recovery Trust is set to distribute approximately $2.2 billion to creditors on March 31, potentially impacting market liquidity.

On a macroeconomic level, the U.S. March non-farm payroll data will be released on Friday, alongside other critical indicators such as the unemployment rate, initial jobless claims, and the ISM index. With ongoing tensions in the Middle East and rising inflation expectations, uncertainty remains regarding the Federal Reserve's policy direction.

Additionally, BNP Paribas plans to launch six crypto ETN products. Several DAO governance votes, token unlocks, and new project launches are also scheduled for this week, including those for SUI, Ethena, and Hyperliquid.

Analysts suggest that the interplay of geopolitical factors, macroeconomic data, and on-chain capital flows could make this week a crucial period for observing short-term trends in the crypto market.
